 {"id":"bd-14v0","title":"Add Windows code signing for bd.exe releases","description":"## Context

Go binaries (including bd.exe) are commonly flagged by antivirus software as false positives due to heuristic detection. See docs/ANTIVIRUS.md for full details.

## Problem

Kaspersky and other AV software flag bd.exe as PDM:Trojan.Win32.Generic, causing it to be quarantined or deleted.

## Solution

Implement code signing for Windows releases using:
1. An EV (Extended Validation) code certificate
2. Integration with GoReleaser to sign Windows binaries during release

## Benefits

- Reduces false positive rates over time as the certificate builds reputation
- Provides tamper verification for users
- Improves SmartScreen trust rating on Windows
- Professional appearance for enterprise users

## Implementation Steps

1. Acquire EV code signing certificate (annual cost ~$300-500)
2. Set up signtool or osslsigncode in release pipeline
3. Update .goreleaser.yml to sign Windows binaries
4. Update checksums to include signed binary hashes
5. Document signing verification in ANTIVIRUS.md

## References

- docs/ANTIVIRUS.md - Current documentation
- bd-t4u1 - Original Kaspersky false positive report
- https://github.com/golang/go/issues/16292 - Go project discussion","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T23:46:48.459177-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T23:54:41.912141-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T23:54:41.912141-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14v0","depends_on_id":"bd-t4u1","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-23T23:47:02.024159-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-164b","title":"Add template support for issue creation","description":"Support creating issues from predefined templates to streamline common workflows like epics, bug reports, or feature proposals.

Example usage:
  bd create --from-template epic "Phase 3 Features"
  bd create --from-template bug "Login failure"
  bd template list
  bd template create epic

Templates should include:
- Pre-filled description structure
- Suggested priority and type
- Common labels
- Design/acceptance criteria sections

Implementation notes:
- Store templates in .beads/templates/ directory
- Support YAML or JSON format
- Ship with built-in templates (epic, bug, feature)
- Allow custom project-specific templates","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-03T18:10:18.985902-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-03T19:56:41.287303-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-03T19:56:41.287303-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-17d5","title":"bd sync false positive: conflict detection triggers on JSON-encoded angle brackets in issue content","description":"The bd sync --import-only command incorrectly detects conflict markers when issue descriptions contain the text '<<<<<<<' or '>>>>>>>' as legitimate content (e.g., documentation about git conflict markers).

**Reproduction:**
1. Create issue with design field containing: 'Read file, extract <<<<<<< / ======= / >>>>>>> markers'
2. Export to JSONL (gets JSON-encoded as \u003c\u003c\u003c...)
3. Commit and push
4. Pull from remote
5. bd sync --import-only fails with: 'Git conflict markers detected in JSONL file'

**Root cause:**
The conflict detection appears to decode JSON before checking for conflict markers, causing false positives when issue content legitimately contains these strings.

**Expected behavior:**
Conflict detection should only trigger on actual git conflict markers (literal '<<<<<<<' bytes in the raw file), not on JSON-encoded content within issue fields.

**Test case:**
VC project at ~/src/dave/vc has vc-85 'JSONL Conflict Parser' which documents conflict parsing and triggers this bug.

**Suggested fixes:**
1. Only scan for literal '<<<<<<<' bytes (not decoded JSON content)
2. Parse JSONL first and only flag unparseable lines
3. Check git merge state (git status) to confirm actual conflict
4. Add --skip-conflict-check flag for override","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-08T13:02:54.730745-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-08T13:07:37.108225-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-08T13:07:37.108225-08:00"}
